title.zh: 同一政策，無標準結果：招生價值觀如何決定免試政策的多元成效
title.en: Same Policy, Different Outcomes: How Institutional Priorities Shape Test-Optional Admissions' Diversity Impact

summary.zh:
standardized tests曾被視為客觀衡量學生智力的工具，卻逐漸成為加劇教育不平等的門檻。近年來，許多大學放棄要求提交測驗成績，試圖打破這種結構性優勢。然而，這項研究揭示了一個驚人的事實：看似相同的免試政策，實際上可能導致完全不同的校園多元成效。關鍵在於各校對「優秀」的定義與財務優先順序。

透過分析國立教育統計中心與College Board的數據，研究發現，雖然免試政策普遍能增加少數族裔學生的入學率，但若校方持續強調數學等量化學術指標，這項效益就會大幅減弱。更關鍵的是，當財務壓力上升時，免試政策的多元效果也會隨之下降。在2025年，當校園多元實踐面臨法律與政治限制時，這項研究提醒我們：若不重新定義「Merit」，免試政策恐怕難以真正推動教育公平。
summary.en: The study addresses a critical question in higher education: do test-optional admissions policies, intended to reduce bias, actually foster campus diversity? Drawing on institutional theory, the authors argue that institutions' definitions of merit and competing financial priorities determine whether such policies yield diversity benefits. Using data from the National Center for Education Statistics and the College Board, they find that while test-optional policies generally increase enrollment of underrepresented minority students, this effect is significantly reduced at institutions that continue to prioritize quantitative academic metrics. Furthermore, when financial pressures rise, the diversity gains from test-optional policies decline. In 2025, as institutions face legal and political restrictions on diversity-related practices, these findings suggest that policies like test-optional admissions may fall short unless institutions fundamentally rethink how they define and reward merit.

keyArticle.title: Same Policy, No Standardized Outcome: How Admissions Values and Institutional Priorities Shape the Effect of Test-Optional Policies on Campus Diversity
